mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports. It is ideal for applications requiring high-performance data processing and querying. Key trade-offs include its column-oriented storage which optimizes read performance but may require more complex data modeling.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.github.io/clickhouse-helm

```

> This is the official repository for ClickHouse Helm charts.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for ClickHouse to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/clickhouse-helm/main/crds/clickhouse.altinity.com_clickhouses.yaml

```

> This CRD is necessary for managing ClickHouse clusters.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> Replace 'clickhouse' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create a ClickHouse Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) to define your ClickHouse cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  config:
    zookeeper:
      nodes:
        - host: zookeeper-0.zookeeper.clickhouse.svc.cluster.local
  pods:
    count: 3
EOF

```

> This example creates a ClickHouse cluster with 3 pods.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  config:
    zookeeper:
      nodes:
        - host: zookeeper-0.zookeeper.clickhouse.svc.cluster.local
  pods:
    count: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> You should see your ClickHouse cluster listed with its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the ClickHouse installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command removes the ClickHouse release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl cp my-clickhouse-0:/var/lib/clickhouse/data /path/to/backup

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> Backup your data from the ClickHouse pod.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Verify the status of the ClickHouse cluster after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the ClickHouse pods with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and review logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Zookeeper connection failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Zookeeper may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure Zookeeper is running and accessible. Check Zookeeper pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to run ClickHouse.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the ClickHouse cluster configuration and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and adjust the configuration or resources as needed.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new SQL functions for analytics, improved performance for large datasets, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy SQL functions that are no longer recommended.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the release notes for any SQL function changes that may affect existing queries.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse' and its status should be 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
